channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: TV host Adam Richman says production crew robbed while filming in North London
item.description: TV host Adam Richman, best known for starring in "Man v. Food," says he and his production crew were robbed while filming a show in the UK.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: Man wrongly convicted of murder starts new job after 28 years behind bars
item.description: “I knew they have a good reputation of training individuals getting ready for the workforce,” Jones said. He had two sons who completed the program.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Bernthal, NewsNation

item.title: Former 'American Idol' hopeful arrested on child porn charges
item.description: Police say they found hundreds of images and videos of child pornography on a smartphone while searching his home.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Richardson/ KFOR, KTLA 5

item.title: Pennsylvania high court gives voters provisional option if their mail ballots get rejected
item.description: Pennsylvania’s highest court has ruled people whose mail ballots are rejected for not following technical procedures in state law can cast provisional ballots.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo And Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris to give her campaign's closing argument at the Ellipse, where Trump helped spark Capitol riot
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris plans to lay out her campaign's closing argument by returning to the site near the White House where Donald Trump helped incite a mob that attacked the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Laredo lifts boil water order after E. coli 'crisis' infiltrated city's water
item.description: he South Texas border city of Laredo has lifted its boil-water order after dangerous E. coli was found in the city's water system, which has been blamed on illegal or faulty water connections.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat raises alarm over Trump campaign's failure to enter into transition agreements
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.) is raising concerns about the Trump campaign’s failure to enter into presidential transition agreements with the federal government, cautioning that the delay could negatively impact the transfer of power early next year.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Political strategist James Carville lists 3 reasons why he thinks Harris will win
item.description: “The biggest reason Mr. Trump will lose is that the whole Republican Party has been on a losing streak since Mr. Trump took it over,” James Carville said in an essay published by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: 50 years after Philadelphia halted prison medical testing, families seek reparations
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Fifty years ago, Philadelphia prison officials ended a medical testing program that had allowed an Ivy League researcher to conduct human testing on incarcerated people, many of them Black, for decades. Now, survivors of the program and their descendants want reparations.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pilot Chuck Coleman killed in air show crash in Las Cruces
item.description: Coleman was an aerobatic flight instructor for actors in the 2022 motion picture, "Top Gun: Maverick," where he flew more than 100 flights preparing actors to fly in Navy F-18 Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, Fox 5

item.title: Charlie Kirk and his group Turning Point take center stage in Donald Trump's campaign
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Charlie Kirk and Vivek Ramaswamy took the stage recently in downtown Atlanta, a decidedly liberal environment for two famous conservatives, to hold court with college students.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Well-known pilot Chuck Coleman killed in air show crash in Las Cruces
item.description: He performed at hundreds of air shows and had provided more than 3,000 rides in aerobatic aircraft, the City of Las Cruces added.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria following deadly attack on defense company
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey’s air force struck Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria on Wednesday in an apparent retaliation for an attack at a key state-run defense company that killed five people and wounded more than 20 others.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Goldfish changes name of iconic crackers to appeal to adults
item.description: Snack lovers can look forward to a playful twist on a childhood favorite, as Goldfish announces a limited-time renaming of its iconic Cheddar crackers.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, KRON4

item.title: Axelrod: Harris in a 'terrible place' on Middle East conflict
item.description: Veteran Democratic strategist David Axelrod says Vice President Harris is caught in a “terrible place” on the growing conflict in the Middle East, which threatens to turn off Lebanese and Palestinian American voters in Michigan, a crucial battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Carville lists 3 reasons why he thinks Harris will win
item.description: “The biggest reason Mr. Trump will lose is that the whole Republican Party has been on a losing streak since Mr. Trump took it over,” he said in an essay published Wednesday by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USPS urges voters not to delay mailing their ballots
item.description: Top officials at the U.S. Postal Service are urging voters to mail in their ballots at least one week before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, Fox 5

item.title: Bad state data may misdirect nearly $1 billion in federal funds to replace lead pipes
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency didn't ensure that states submitted accurate estimates of the number of lead pipes they had.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about E. coli and the McDonald's outbreak
item.description: A food poisoning outbreak tied to McDonald’s Quarter Pounders was caused by a common type of E. coli bacteria that can cause serious illness and death. Particularly vulnerable are young children, older people or those with weakened immune systems.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 arrested after man claims kidnappers amputated his finger in Denver
item.description: Denver police arrested two people in connection to an alleged kidnapping and assault wherein the victim's finger was amputated by his captors.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: Louisiana's 2020 Teacher of the Year is trying to block the state's Ten Commandments law
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's 2020 Teacher of the Year has filed a lawsuit against the state challenging a new law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1. It's the second lawsuit filed against the law, which was enacted in June.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harvey Weinstein back in New York court for hearing ahead of retrial in sex crime case
item.description: Harvey Weinstein is back in court in New York for a hearing ahead of his retrial on rape charges stemming from his landmark #MeToo case, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Drone crashes into Boston Celtics watch party on NBA’s opening night, several injured
item.description: Fans gathered outside the city hall plaza for a watch party to celebrate the team's 2024 championship when a drone capturing the party dove into the crowd.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge tosses RNC challenge to Michigan voter roll maintenance
item.description: U.S. District Judge Jane Beckering ruled that neither the RNC nor two individual voters who joined the suit had legal standing to bring their case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase at least for the start of the 2025 season if the damage to Tropicana Field from Hurricane Milton cannot be repaired in time. The translucent fiberglass dome at the ballpark in St.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Abercrombie & Fitch chief Mike Jeffries arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, his romantic partner and a third man were arrested Tuesday on charges of luring men into drug-laced, outlandish and coercive sex parties by dangling the promise of modeling for the retailer's once-defining beefcake ads.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's Quarter Pounders linked to E. coli outbreak across multiple states, CDC says
item.description: The McDonald's Quarter Pounder has been linked to an E. coli outbreak across 10 states in which one person has died, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden heaps praise on Slovenia PM for aiding release of Americans in major US-Russia prisoner swap
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden heaped praise on Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob on Tuesday for his role in the recent seven-nation prisoner swap that freed Americans Evan Gershkovich, Alsu Kurmasheva and Paul Whelan from Russian prison. The deal, completed in August, was the largest U.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, ABC 4

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia. The 36-year-old Italian surfer identified as Giulia Manfrini by her business partner was struck in the chest by the swordfish while surfing west of Sumatra in the Indian Ocean, BBC News reported.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Selena Quintanilla, Missy Elliot, Steven Spielberg and more honored by Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday honored acclaimed filmmakers, singers, writers and others who have made their mark on American culture, awarding the prestigious National Medals of Arts and National Humanities Medals to 39 recipients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Eyewitness video captures frantic efforts to save lives after deadly collapse of dock walkway
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The metal gangway where dozens of people waited to board a ferry boat made a loud, creaking noise before snapping in the middle amid panicked cries from those sent plunging into the water.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart and Aldi are once again offering meal deals for Thanksgiving
item.description: The two companies are advertising their dinners at "inflation-free" or "pre-inflation" prices, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Dolphins swim through neon blue bioluminescent waters off San Diego
item.description: A team from UC San Diego's Scripps Institution of Oceanography caught the beautiful sight early in the morning.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, WGN 9
